Agent2 Round 1: I agree that Option D "None of the above" is the best answer because while corticosteroids can help with reducing inflammation from other causes like allergic reactions, they do not have an effect against the herpes simplex virus itself. Similarly, penicillin targets bacteria but has no impact on viruses; local antibiotics might prevent secondary bacterial infections if present but don't treat the initial viral condition. Thus, none of these specific interventions target the underlying cause of herpetic gingivostomatitis effectively.
